seoTitle: How to Use List View in Airtable Interfaces
metaDescription: Build a hierarchical List View in Airtable's Interface Designer with nested records, grouping, and color-coded priorities for dashboards.
intro: List View inside Airtable's Interface Designer is built for hierarchical data — nested project tasks, linked sub-records, or anything with layered structure — displayed in a clean interactive dashboard. This guide covers setting up the hierarchy, customizing each row's fields, and applying grouping and color rules to make it scannable.

steps.heading: Open Interface Designer
steps.body: Launch the Interface Designer hub from your Airtable workspace.

steps.heading: Create a List View page
steps.body: Start a new interface page and select the specialized List View layout.

steps.heading: Choose your source table and hierarchy
steps.body: Pick your target source table and define the hierarchical data levels it should display.

steps.heading: Map linked records for nesting
steps.body: Map linked records to create nested dropdowns, useful for complex project tracking with sub-items.

steps.heading: Customize row fields
steps.body: Choose which fields display on each row, such as statuses, owners, and due dates.

steps.heading: Group and color-code, then publish
steps.body: Use grouping to cluster records by category or priority, apply conditional color formatting to highlight urgent items, adjust permissions, and publish.

faq.question: What kind of data works best in List View?
faq.answer: Data with a natural hierarchy — like projects with nested tasks or categories with sub-items — is the best fit for List View's nested structure.

faq.question: Can I color-code list items by priority?
faq.answer: Yes, conditional color formatting lets you visually highlight records like urgent items based on field values.

faq.question: How is List View different from Grid View in the base itself?
faq.answer: List View is an Interface Designer layout meant for dashboards and nested browsing, while Grid View is the standard spreadsheet-like editing view within the base.

faq.question: Who can see a published List View interface?
faq.answer: You control this through the viewer permissions you set before publishing, ranging from just yourself to your entire team.
